title: build,config: add support for `--unsetlabel`
body:
Just like `--unsetenv` following flag allows to unset image label.

```release-note
build: add support for `--unsetlabel`
config: add support for `--unsetlabel`
```

Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/5063

comments.body:
Would it make sense to add either glob support or an "all" option?

--unsetlabel=all
or 
--unsetlabel=*
@rhatdan I don't think its possible because both `buildkit` and `buildah` allows setting label with keys `*` and `all`, may we need a different flag for this use-case.
You could always check if the string was an exact match, otherwise treat it as a keyword.  Since it is unlikely that users would just set a label of * or all

> You could always check if the string was an exact match, otherwise treat it as a keyword. Since it is unlikely that users would just set a label of * or all

I am okay with it if everybody agrees with it, although this will leave a edge case intentionally i.e users will not be able only remove key if they are `*` or `all`. @nalind @containers/buildah-maintainers WDYT ?

If you want to support removing all labels, I think that would be better to achieve via an explicit call (i.e. https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5061) instead of overriding a specific label with a "remove all" functionality. In my opinion, the removal of labels should be very intentional -- and that requests should be very explicit. By making the behavior of the removal of specific labels hinge on the "best matched" label, it introduces uncertainty in the removal process that unintended labels can be removed if a parent image changes _their_ labels.

Those are different things, and I'm not aware of any flags that do wildcard matching except when dealing with filenames - capabilities take "all" as an alias, but I _think_ that's it.  For some reason we added `--unsetenv` to `commit` but not `config`, where `--env` is, so we're actually talking about `config`, `commit`, and `build` here.

> @nalind i have extended `unsetlabel` to `commit` and `config` as well.

Please don't add this to `commit`.  It fits better with what `config` has always been doing, and I consider our muddying things by putting `--unsetenv` in `commit` instead `config` to have been a mistake.  But maybe I'm in a minority there.
I am fine with no glob and only "all" to match --cap-drop, --cap-add
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *arewm*, *flouthoc*, *nalind* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc,nalind] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

/lgtm
Still think we should have support for keyword "all", but will not block.
